findings.description: args.output (line 27) is passed directly to BacktestRunner.run() without path validation. If the BacktestRunner writes to this path without sanitization, a caller could use path traversal (e.g., '../../etc/critical_file') to overwrite arbitrary files. Mitigation depends on BacktestRunner internal validation — audit BacktestRunner.run() for path sanitization.

json.dumps(report, indent=2) at line 35 prints the full backtest report to stdout. If report contains sensitive data from the shadow database (shadow names, strategy details, performance metrics), this leaks to console logs. In production, ensure stdout is captured and not exposed.

Error handling at lines 28-33 distinguishes ValueError/FileNotFoundError (user error) from generic Exception (unexpected). Both print to stderr and return exit code 1. No sensitive data leaked in error messages — only the exception string.

notes: 37-line entry point with minimal attack surface. Path traversal risk depends on BacktestRunner internal validation — recommended to add os.path.abspath() + prefix check here as defense-in-depth. The stdout leak is a configuration concern (ensure log capture in production), not a code vulnerability.
